text.title: Architecture Proposal Document Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Architecture Proposal Document Template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One decision per proposal; bundling two interleaved decisions makes both worse.
rule.rationale: Bundled proposals force the committee to package-deal; per-decision proposals keep options clean.

rule.statement: Constraints list ≥3 items with sources; budget, SLA, regulatory mentioned explicitly when applicable.
rule.rationale: Constraint omission is the root cause of post-decision rework; explicit constraints front-load the trade-offs.

rule.statement: ≥3 credible options including 'do nothing'; single-option proposals are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-option proposals are decisions disguised as proposals; the committee cannot deliberate without alternatives.

rule.statement: Rollback section is required, naming gate criteria and decision authority.
rule.rationale: Rollback-as-afterthought leaks into incident time; up-front rollback design prevents this.

rule.statement: Single named owner accountable for the decision and its outcome; pronoun ownership rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need a named human to chase.
